panels.composition: Warmly lit hotel lounge; classmates around a table, some smiling, others pensive. Michael among them.
panels.action: The group chats, then falls into a contemplative silence. Sunlight streams through windows.

panels.composition: Angela gestures, half-smiling; soft window light behind her.

dialogue.text: Life turned out so differently, didn’t it?

panels.composition: Nathan rubbing his temple, concern in his eyes.

dialogue.text: It has. But notice—when things change, we don’t want to change with them.

panels.composition: Nathan's hands, rubbing his temple, then clasping.

dialogue.text: We’re all trying to cope with unexpected changes… and honestly, none of us know how.

panels.composition: Michael leaning forward, hands wrapped around a coffee mug, a thoughtful half-smile.

dialogue.text: I used to be terrified of change too.

storyboard_page.page_turn_hook: But Michael had a secret—a simple story that changed everything.

composition.composition_notes: A three-row layout leads the eye from a wide group setup to character close-ups, anchoring the page turn on Michael's line.

panels.composition: Michael’s eyes brightening, a slight smile forming as he remembers the story.

dialogue.text: Let me tell you a story that changed everything for me.

panels.composition: Michael gesturing with open hands; Angela and Carlos visible behind him, tilting their heads with curiosity.

dialogue.text: It shifted my view—from losing something to gaining something. It showed me how to move.

panels.composition: Michael leans in, voice earnest.

dialogue.text: At first I was annoyed by its simplicity… Then I saw the four characters were parts of me. I chose which one to act like.

panels.composition: Carlos raises an eyebrow, intrigued.

dialogue.text: What’s the story?

panels.composition: Michael smiling, a twinkle in his eye.
panels.action: Michael takes a deep breath, a soft smile playing on his lips.

dialogue.text: It’s called "Who Moved My Cheese?"

storyboard_page.page_turn_hook: A strange world of cheese and mazes awaits...

composition.composition_notes: Opening with a striking close-up, then a pair of reaction shots, landing on Michael's soft smile as the page-turn beat.

panels.composition: A sprawling labyrinth of dark stone corridors, dotted with glowing cheese wedges.
panels.action: Maze corridors stretch into infinity, golden light emanating from distant cheese stations.
panels.narration: Cheese is a metaphor for whatever you want in life—a job, a relationship, money, health. The Maze is where you look for it—your workplace, your community.

panels.composition: A radiant wedge of Cheese against a starry void; tiny symbols of a heart, a house, and a coin orbit around it.
panels.action: The cheese glows warmly, symbols slowly turning.

panels.composition: A single long corridor with a bright cheese station at the end.
panels.narration: In the Maze, four tiny characters search for their special Cheese.

composition.composition_notes: A wide establishing shot sets the scene, followed by a symbolic detail and the explanatory page-turn introducing the Maze characters.

panels.composition: Maze corridor; four small silhouettes move through shadow.
panels.action: Four tiny figures navigate the dark paths.
panels.narration: Once upon a time, four little characters lived in the Maze: two mice and two Littlepeople.

panels.composition: Sniff, a small grey mouse, nose high, sniffing the air.
panels.action: Sniff's nose twitches rapidly.
panels.narration: Sniff, who sniffs out change early.

panels.composition: Scurry, a sleek dark-grey mouse in a running pose, tiny red shoes dangling from his neck.
panels.action: Scurry's legs are blurred with speed lines.
panels.narration: Scurry, who scurries into action.

panels.composition: Hem, a round Littleperson in a pale blue bathrobe and fuzzy bunny slippers, arms crossed, frowning.
panels.action: Hem huffs and crosses his arms tighter.
panels.narration: Hem, who denies and resists change, fearing it will be worse.

panels.composition: Haw, a lean Littleperson with tousled brown hair, worried eyes, running shoes dangling unused around his neck.
panels.action: Haw bites his lip, glancing around nervously.
panels.narration: Haw, who learns to adapt when he sees change can lead to something better.

panels.composition: The four characters side by side in a panel, with labels ‘Sniff’, ‘Scurry’, ‘Hem’, ‘Haw’ in stylized text.
panels.action: They stand in a tableau.
panels.narration: These four represent the simple and complex parts inside all of us, no matter our age or background.

composition.composition_notes: Three rows of paired panels introduce each character, culminating in a symbolic group shot anchoring the page turn.

panels.composition: All four characters running through the Maze, wearing jogging suits and running shoes.
panels.action: They race along a corridor, legs pumping.
panels.narration: Every day, they each put on their running gear and raced into the Maze to find their favorite cheese.

panels.composition: Sniff and Scurry arriving at Cheese Station C, taking off shoes, hanging them around their necks.
panels.action: The mice tie shoes together and hang them, ready for quick use.
panels.narration: Sniff and Scurry kept their running shoes handy, ready to move at any moment.

panels.composition: Hem and Haw arriving leisurely, taking off running shoes, putting on fuzzy slippers, relaxing.
panels.action: Hem tosses aside his shoes, Haw stretches and yawns.
panels.narration: Hem and Haw grew comfortable, hanging up their jogging suits and donning slippers.

panels.composition: Hem lounging on a pile of cheese, mouth full, looking smug.
panels.action: Hem pats his belly.

dialogue.text: There's enough Cheese here to last us forever.

panels.composition: Haw drawing a picture of cheese on the wall with a smile, Hem asleep nearby.
panels.action: Haw sketches a large cheese wedge, humming.
panels.narration: They decorated the walls with cheese sayings, feeling secure and entitled.

composition.composition_notes: A two-one-two rhythm moves from a wide group action to a central moment of complacency, climaxing with Haw's entitled sketching as the page-turn beat.

panels.composition: Cheese Station C, piles of cheese are visibly shrinking.
panels.action: Crumbs and empty spots appear.
panels.narration: But the cheese supply was dwindling... and Sniff and Scurry noticed.

panels.composition: Sniff and Scurry inspecting the pile, exchanging a glance.
panels.action: Sniff sniffs, Scurry's ears twitch.

panels.composition: Sniff's nose, Scurry's eyes; they nod to each other.
panels.action: With a determined squeak, they lace up their running shoes.
panels.narration: They were not surprised. They had prepared for change.

panels.composition: Motion lines as Sniff and Scurry dash out of the station into the dark Maze.
panels.action: The two mice sprint away, tiny shoes kicking up dust.
panels.narration: They didn't overthink—they just moved, quickly off to find New Cheese.

panels.composition: Empty Cheese Station C, shelves bare, a single forgotten crumb.
panels.action: Silence lingers in the empty chamber.
panels.narration: Later that day, Hem and Haw would arrive... unprepared.

composition.composition_notes: A three-panel setup pinpoints the mice's awareness, then a dynamic two-panel row drives the page-turn with the empty chamber cliffhanger.

panels.composition: Hem and Haw arriving at Cheese Station C, looking confused.
panels.action: Haw enters hesitantly; Hem pushes past, expecting cheese.

panels.composition: Hem's face twists in rage.
panels.action: His jaw drops, veins pop.

dialogue.text: No Cheese! Who moved my Cheese?!

panels.composition: Hem throwing his arms up, shouting; Haw frozen, hands over mouth.
panels.action: Hem stomps; Haw trembles.

dialogue.text: This isn’t fair! Someone has to put it back!

panels.composition: Haw's eyes wide with disbelief, a hint of worry.

dialogue.text: Where are Sniff and Scurry?

panels.composition: Hem scoffs, arms crossed, while Haw looks toward the Maze exit.
panels.action: Hem rolls his eyes.

dialogue.text: They're just mice. We're smarter. We should figure this out.

composition.composition_notes: The page builds from entry to denial, with Hem's dismissive eye-roll anchoring the page-turn.

panels.composition: Haw rubbing his chin, thoughtful; Hem pacing.
panels.action: Haw glances at the exit.

dialogue.text: Maybe we should… move. Find new Cheese.

panels.composition: Hem stopping, arms crossed, face stubborn.
panels.action: Hem plants his feet.

dialogue.text: No! I like it here. It's comfortable. I'm not going.

panels.composition: Haw looking torn, hope fading.
panels.action: His shoulders slump.
panels.narration: Hem's fear paralyzed them both. Day after day, they returned to the empty station, hoping the cheese would come back.

panels.composition: Haw standing alone at the edge of Cheese Station C, gazing into the dark Maze corridor, a small rock in his hand.
panels.action: He takes a deep breath, then writes on the wall: ‘What would you do if you weren't afraid?’
panels.narration: After many hungry days, Haw finally laughed at his own foolishness.

panels.composition: Haw turning his back on Hem, now wearing his running shoes, a backpack on.
panels.action: He glances back at Hem, who sits stubbornly in the corner.
panels.narration: He knew he had to venture out alone. Hem refused to budge, a cautionary figure of what happens when you resist change.

storyboard_page.page_turn_hook: One small step into the unknown...

composition.composition_notes: A solo panel sets Haw's internal conflict, then a pair of reactions before his decisive turn; the page-turn captures his resolve to leave Hem.

panels.composition: Haw jogging slowly into a dark corridor, fear visible on his face.
panels.action: He shivers, but presses forward.
panels.narration: Haw stepped into the unknown, fear gripping his heart. But he wrote on the wall: ‘If You Do Not Change, You Can Become Extinct.’

panels.composition: Haw's face breaking into a smile, laughing at himself.
panels.action: He claps a hand over his mouth, chuckling.
panels.narration: He laughed at his own fearful imaginings, realizing his fears were making things worse.

panels.composition: Haw writing on the wall with chalk, a section of text: ‘What Would You Do If You Weren't Afraid?’
panels.action: He carves the letters, then stands back.
panels.narration: He kept writing, leaving a trail of wisdom.

panels.composition: Haw moving with more energy, a cool breeze ruffles his hair, a smile.
panels.action: He runs now, arms pumping.
panels.narration: He discovered that letting go and trusting what lay ahead nourished his soul. As he moved beyond his fear, he felt free—the Maze no longer seemed terrifying.

panels.composition: Haw's hands holding a mental image bubble: himself surrounded by cheeses.
panels.action: He closes his eyes, envisioning a feast.
panels.narration: He used visualization, picturing himself enjoying new cheese, which spurred him on.

panels.composition: Haw crouching, writing another message: ‘The Quicker You Let Go Of Old Cheese, The Sooner You Find New Cheese.’
panels.action: He writes quickly.
panels.narration: He passed a sign he had written for Hem, just in case.

composition.composition_notes: A triptych of paired panels tracks Haw's journey, ending on his written wisdom for Hem as the page-turn beat.

panels.composition: Haw finding a small cheese station, but only crumbs remain.
panels.action: He picks up a tiny cheese crumb, disappointment on his face.
panels.narration: Haw found empty stations and only scraps, but he wrote: ‘Old Beliefs Do Not Lead You To New Cheese.’

panels.composition: Haw rounding a corner to see a huge Cheese Station N, laden with varieties of cheese.
panels.action: His eyes widen.
panels.narration: Then, at last, he stumbled upon Cheese Station N—the greatest supply of cheese he had ever seen.

panels.composition: Inside Cheese Station N, Haw sees Sniff and Scurry already feasting, their bellies round.
panels.action: Sniff nods, Scurry waves a paw. Haw gasps with joy.
panels.narration: His old friends were there, having thrived by moving early.

panels.composition: Haw jumping into a pile of cheese, laughing.
panels.action: He plunges into a mound, cheese wedges flying.
panels.narration: Haw celebrated, writing on the largest wall: ‘Move With The Cheese And Enjoy It!’

panels.composition: Haw standing before a wall covered with glowing handwriting lessons, Sniff and Scurry watching.
panels.action: He steps back, proud of the trail he left.
panels.narration: His journey complete, he knew change was an adventure, not a threat.

storyboard_page.page_turn_hook: Back in Chicago, the story settled in...

composition.composition_notes: A full-width transition leads to a breathtaking discovery and celebration, with Haw's final realization anchoring the page-turn.

panels.composition: Michael closing his eyes, ending the story; classmates around the table listening intently.
panels.action: The group remains silent for a moment.
panels.narration: Michael finished the tale. The lounge felt heavier with meaning.

panels.composition: Angela's face, eyes wide, a slight smile.

dialogue.text: So… which character am I?

panels.composition: Carlos leaning forward, hand on chin.

dialogue.text: I know I’ve been Hem — wanting to stay in familiar territory.

panels.composition: Michael gesturing, a kind but firm expression.

dialogue.text: The secret: anticipate change like Sniff, act quickly like Scurry, overcome fear like Haw. Resist like Hem, and you stay stuck.

panels.composition: Michael stands, the classmates glance at each other.
panels.action: He looks around the table.

dialogue.text: This little story… it’s more than a fable. It’s a mirror reflecting our own lives.

panels.composition: The group in the lounge, ghostly silhouettes of Sniff, Scurry, Hem, Haw hovering behind each person.
panels.action: They nod slowly, some chuckling.
panels.narration: And then Michael posed a question that hung in the air...

panels.composition: A hand raising among the group, with the words ‘Who here is afraid of change?’ appearing; then an overlay of many hands rising.
panels.action: Reluctantly, one hand goes up. Then Michael asks another question and nearly all hands shoot up, laughter erupts.
panels.narration: The real journey was just beginning.

composition.composition_notes: A seven-panel finale assembles the group's reactions in rows of three, two, then two, culminating in a symbolic wide panel that teases the next chapter.
